title: Quiz: Capstone Project
description: Test your understanding of the quantum finance platform integration

questions.question: What is the purpose of the ExecutionMode enum in the platform?

options.text: To specify whether to use quantum, classical, or hybrid execution

questions.explanation: ExecutionMode determines whether the platform executes using quantum algorithms, classical algorithms, or hybrid mode with automatic fallback.

questions.question: Why does the platform use a modular architecture?

options.text: To make the code more complex
To enable independent development, testing, and replacement of components

questions.explanation: Modular architecture allows each component (portfolio, risk, pricing) to be developed, tested, and updated independently, improving maintainability and flexibility.

questions.question: What happens when quantum execution quality falls below the threshold in hybrid mode?

options.text: The user is prompted to retry

questions.explanation: In hybrid mode, when the quality score falls below the configured threshold (default 0.8), the system automatically falls back to classical execution for reliable results.

questions.question: What is tracked in the ExecutionResult dataclass?

options.text: Module type, success, mode used, result, execution time, and errors

questions.explanation: ExecutionResult comprehensively tracks the module, success status, execution mode, actual result, timing, quantum shots used, and any error messages for full observability.

questions.question: The InvestmentWorkflow class demonstrates how to chain multiple quantum modules together.
questions.explanation: True. The InvestmentWorkflow class shows how to orchestrate portfolio optimization, risk analysis, and option pricing modules into a cohesive end-to-end analysis pipeline.

questions.question: What does the AnalyticsDashboard calculate?

options.text: Platform metrics including execution counts, success rates, and quality scores

questions.explanation: The AnalyticsDashboard aggregates comprehensive metrics from all module execution logs, including total executions, quantum vs classical ratios, success rates, and average quality scores.

questions.question: What is a 'blocking item' in the ProductionReadinessChecker?

options.text: A required item that is not yet completed
An item that speeds up execution

questions.explanation: A blocking item is a required checklist item that hasn't been completed yet, preventing production deployment until it is addressed.

questions.question: Which modules are integrated in the capstone platform? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The capstone platform integrates portfolio optimization, risk analysis (VaR), and derivatives pricing modules. Social media analysis is not part of the quantum finance platform.
